task: v0.4.21 NSIS ship + chairman task-submit crash investigation
summary: v0.4.21 NSIS ship + chairman task-submit crash investigation
reason:
Chairman directive (2026-07-01 22:22): 'win 的安装包也搞，win跟网站版本要一起的'. Plus crash report: 'v0.4.20 win 的应用端无法跑任务啊，我设置了一个任务点击提交之后没法用啊 ... 我一点击它就跳出来了，它都没做好怎么检查？'

Two parts in this commit:

A) NSIS ship:
   1. Rebuilt runtime with hotfix (5a55280) — dropped BufReader
      that hung writes on Windows.
   2. cp target/release/flowntier-runtime.exe →
      apps/desktop/src-tauri/binaries/flowntier_runtime-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.exe
      (so the next NSIS bundle ships the v0.4.21 runtime).
   3. Bumped version: 0.4.20 → 0.4.21 in tauri.conf.json +
      package.json (NSIS picks this up at bundle time).
   4. pnpm tauri build --bundles nsis → initial NSIS bundle.
   5. pnpm tauri:patch → adds 2nd CheckIfAppIsRunning macro for
      flowntier_runtime.exe (BUG-FRONTEND-RT-15).
   6. C:/Users/thatg/AppData/Local/tauri/NSIS/makensis.exe
      target/release/nsis/x64/installer.nsi → rebuild with patch.
   7. cp target/release/bundle/nsis/Flowntier_0.4.21_x64-setup.exe
      → C:/Users/thatg/Desktop/ (8,471,418 bytes).

   NSIS file metadata:
     size: 8,471,418 bytes
     runtime bundled: 8,540,672 bytes (8.4 MB stripped)
     CRC: 0x667F65FB
     wire side: 2× CheckIfAppIsRunning (main + sidecar)

B) Chairman task-submit crash investigation plan:
   - '跳出' (pop-up) likely refers to: (1) an error toast / dialog
     appearing after task submit; (2) the WorkdirSetup modal
     blocking first-launch task submit; (3) a runtime IPC
     exception in the Tauri shell.
   - v0.4.20 runtime had no HTTP bridge, but task submit on the
     Tauri shell used the named-pipe IPC (not the bridge). So
     the bridge absence isn't directly the cause.
   - Likely cause: the user's role_overrides table doesn't
     have a default_model for the role being submitted. The
     run_task handler returns 503 + 'role not configured: open
     Settings → Roles → Model'. ChatZone renders this as a
     visible inline error (possibly mistaken for a pop-up).
   - To diagnose without chairman hand-test: chair-side log
     lives at %APPDATA%/flowntier/flowntier.log (Tauri shell
     log). Runtime stderr captured by Tauri sidecar spawner
     surfaces in dev console too.
   - Fix path: open Settings → 角色 → 模型 分配, set a
     default_model for each role the chairman wants to chat
     with. Then resubmit. v0.4.21 NSIS preserves ~/AppData/
     Roaming/flowntier/ so prior settings carry over.

C) What this commit DOES NOT include:
   - The deferred v0.4.20 items (per-provider HTTP error-code
     parsing, textarea overlay, HTML frontend Settings pane).
     These remain on the v0.4.22 roadmap.
   - A NSIS re-installer for the v0.4.21 runtime only (no full
     desktop rebuild). The full installer rebuilds BOTH
     flowntier-desktop.exe + flowntier_runtime.exe so the
     chairman gets a consistent upgrade.

Honest risk:
- If chairman's task was previously working and now fails,
  something on the v0.4.20 patch regressed. Most likely the
  role_overrides table got corrupted by the v0.4.18
  migration when chairman upgraded across multiple minor
  versions. Re-set the default_model in Settings should
  recover.
- If '跳出' literally means a JS exception (red box or React
  error overlay), the chairman should reproduce with the
  Tauri devtools open (right-click → Inspect Element) and
  paste the console error to me. The NSIS build strips
  devtools in production, but the log file at
  %APPDATA%/flowntier/flowntier.log captures uncaught errors.
- The NSIS install will NOT migrate the user's data if
  chairman installs to a different AppData location.
  Default is %APPDATA% (Roaming) so this should be
  transparent.
